1	{
2	  "body": "The term UBERON:0002346 \"neurectoderm\" also has a synonym \"neuroectoderm.\" However, neuroectoderm seems more common amongst the linked cross references, and I also see more results when exact searching Pubmed for neuroectoderm. Are there any reasons why neurectoderm is preferred by UBERON over neuroectoderm?\n\n\n\n",
3	  "comments": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd871r0pP",
6	      "author": {
7	        "login": "cmungall"
8	      },
9	      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
10	      "body": "@dragon-ai-agent please swap label and exact synonym, reference this issue\n\nalso please open an issue on the cell-ontology repo to make a corresponding change to any cl terms (you should find them yourself first to save the cl editors the work)",
11	      "createdAt": "2026-03-24T22:44:47Z",
12	      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
13	      "isMinimized": false,
14	      "minimizedReason": "",
15	      "reactionGroups": [],

17	      "viewerDidAuthor": true
18	    }
19	  ],
20	  "title": "UBERON:0002346 \"neurectoderm\" vs \"neuroectoderm\"",
21	  "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3682"
22	}

The only CL term related to this in UBERON's edit file is **CL:0000133 "neurectodermal cell"** (referenced via a `has_part` relationship from UBERON:0002346). A corresponding label/synonym swap (and update of the `has_relational_adjective` if present) should be requested in the cell-ontology repo.

I'll open a corresponding issue in the cell-ontology repo with this list so the CL editors don't have to re-do the search.
